summary: Phellodendron amurense is a species of tree in the family Rutaceae, commonly called the Amur cork tree. It is a major source of huáng bò ( Chinese: 黄 柏 or 黄 檗 ), one of the 50 fundamental herbs used in traditional Chinese medicine. The Ainu people used its fruit, called "shikerebe-ni" (in Ainu, sikerpe ), as a painkiller. It is known as hwangbyeok in Korean and (キハダ) kihada in Japanese.
